resources.string: Visit this address on your phone or computer (local network only):
Or, scan this QR code with your phone:
How do I get these credentials?
Open Home Assistant in your browser or mobile app
Click on your user profile (bottom left)
Create a new token named \'Bing-Bong\' (or any name you prefer)
Copy the token and paste it via the easy setup link, or write it manually
The token will be encrypted and stored securely on your device only. It never leaves your TV except to communicate directly with your Home Assistant. It will be available for 10 years.
Enter just the IP address of your Home Assistant (e.g., 192.168.1.10) or add http:// or https:// before it. Do not enter any port.
To detect remote control buttons, please enable the Bing-Bong service in your system\'s Accessibility settings.
To display the quick bar over other apps, please grant the overlay permission.
No saved entities found. Please go to the \'Entities\' screen to import them first.
Rename, change icon, or delete your Saved Entities that were imported from Home Assistant
No quick bars created.\nPress the button below to add one.

Home Assistant quick controls for Android TV.

Bing-Bong is free software (GPL-3.0), based on QuickBars by Omri Peretz (github.com/Trooped/QuickBars).
Source code: github.com/zaieeeem/QuickBars
This service allows Bing-Bong to detect remote control button presses to launch your quick bars from any screen.
Select an item and press CENTER to toggle move mode
Start by selecting the entities you want to use. You can always change your selections later. Press Continue when you\'re finished.

To display your quick bars on top of other apps, Bing-Bong requires the \"Display over other apps\" permission in system settings.\n\nThis is a core feature of the app and is only used to display the quick bar when you activate a trigger key.
Quick bars won\'t appear over other apps
\"Display over other apps\" permission is required to display quick bars
Find Bing-Bong in the list and enable it
To let Bing-Bong appear on top of other apps, the \"Display over other apps\" permission is required.

To use your remote control buttons as trigger keys, Bing-Bong requires permission to listen for key presses. This is handled by Android\'s Accessibility Service. \nPlease enable "Bing-Bong" in accessibility settings to activate your trigger keys.
Trigger keys won\'t respond to remote buttons
Accessibility Service is required for trigger keys to work.
Scroll down to \"SERVICES\", find Bing-Bong and enable it
To allow remote buttons to work as trigger keys, the Accessibility Service must be enabled in settings.

What This Permission Allows Bing-Bong to Do: 

• Listen for physical button presses from your remote control. 
• Let you assign a new remote button by capturing it in the trigger setup screen. 
• Activate your actions (like displaying a quick bar or toggling a light) when a configured trigger is detected.


What This Permission DOES NOT Allow (Your Privacy Guarantee): 

• It cannot read any content on your screen (passwords, messages, etc.). 
• It cannot see what you are typing in other apps. 
• It cannot collect or store any of your personal data. 
• It cannot perform any actions you have not configured.


The app only uses this service for its core "trigger key" feature and has been declared to Google as a tool for general users, not one intended for disability assistance.
Unable to open overlay settings. Please navigate manually: Settings > Apps > Special app access > Draw over other apps and enable Bing-Bong

This app uses the Accessibility Service API to provide its core functionality. The Accessibility Service is required to detect and intercept specific hardware key presses (e.g., from your remote control) that you configure as "trigger keys." This allows the app to launch your quick bars or control your Home Assistant devices even when the app is in the background.
Bing-Bong does not use the Accessibility Service to collect any user data, see your personal information, or read the content of your screen. It is used solely for the key press trigger feature.

Home Assistant
 • Name: %1$s
 • URL: %2$s

 Bing-Bong Pairing
 • Integration ID: %4$s
 • Paired: %5$s
 • Connected now: %6$s
Pairing cleared. You can re-pair from Home Assistant.
